name: Nicaragua
experiences: San Juan del Sur surf sunsets
avoid: May to November is wetter across the country, so ferry crossings to Ometepe, Playa Maderas road access, and long chicken-bus days are less comfortable and more delay-prone.
Semana Santa, Christmas, and New Year push up boutique-hotel rates in Granada and San Juan del Sur while volcano shuttles and ferries fill faster than usual.
Intercity buses are cheap but slow once you start stacking multiple legs, so avoid packing León, Ometepe, and San Juan del Sur into too few days.
stopoverNote: Managua is the practical flight hub, and it is also the easiest place to stitch together Granada, León, and the southbound Rivas-Ometepe-San Juan del Sur loop with buses, taxis, and ferries.

cities.name: Managua
cities.notes: Capital gateway for markets, lakefront walks, airport overnights, and the easiest launch point for Masaya Volcano, Granada buses, and onward intercity transfers.

cities.name: Granada
cities.notes: Best colonial base in Nicaragua for cathedral views, horse-cart streets, lake islets, and easy day trips to Masaya or Laguna de Apoyo.

cities.name: León
cities.notes: Historic university city for revolutionary history, the giant white cathedral rooftop, strong food stops, and Cerro Negro volcano-boarding departures.

cities.name: Ometepe Island
cities.notes: Twin-volcano island in Lake Nicaragua for ferry arrivals, jungle hikes, springs, beaches, and relaxed stays around Moyogalpa, Balgüe, or Santo Domingo.

cities.name: San Juan del Sur
cities.notes: Pacific beach town for surf shuttles to Playa Maderas, catamaran sunsets, bayfront dinners, and an easy final stop before heading back toward Managua.

itinerary.sim: Claro or Tigo prepaid SIM/eSIM plans are the easiest pickup on arrival; expect roughly ₹700–₹1,400 for a short-stay data pack. Coverage is solid in Managua, Granada, and León, but it weakens on Ometepe roads and some San Juan del Sur beach runs, so download offline maps before bus and ferry days.

Managua.name: Managua
Managua.note: Use Managua as a short arrival or departure buffer; rely on inDrive or hotel taxis after dark and fold Masaya Volcano into this stop if you want the cleanest logistics.

activities.name: Plaza de la Revolución and the Old Cathedral of Managua exterior walk
Puerto Salvador Allende boardwalk on Lake Managua
Masaya Volcano National Park entry or standard sunset crater visit
activities.tip: Official foreign-adult rates are about 150 NIO for daytime entry or US$10 for the regular night visit.

activities.name: Masaya Handicraft Market stop on the return drive

Granada.name: Granada
Granada.note: Stay near Parque Colón or Calle La Calzada so you can walk most evenings; horse-carriage traffic is part of the atmosphere, but the old core is easiest to enjoy early and late in the day.

activities.name: Catedral de Granada interior and Parque Colón walk
Islets of Granada shared boat tour on Lake Nicaragua
Convento y Museo San Francisco
Calle La Calzada evening café and music strip
Iglesia de La Merced bell-tower climb for city views
activities.tip: Granada Cathedral itself is free; the paid panoramic tower climb most travelers do is La Merced.

activities.name: ChocoMuseo Granada workshop or tasting session
Laguna de Apoyo beach-club day pass or swimming stop
Photo loop past Iglesia de Xalteva and quiet side streets

León.name: León
León.note: León is hot and very walkable, so front-load cathedral and rooftop visits in the morning, then save longer museum stops or restaurant breaks for the hottest part of the afternoon.

days.theme: UNESCO Cathedral, Revolution History & Street Life

activities.name: León Cathedral rooftop visit
activities.tip: Foreign rooftop admission is about 115 NIO, usually including shoe covers for the white dome walk.

activities.name: Basílica Catedral interior and Plaza de la Independencia circuit
Museo de la Revolución guided visit
Iglesia La Recolección and mural-lined streets
Cerro Negro volcano-boarding half-day tour from León
Fundación Ortiz-Gurdián art museum
Mercado Central fruit or vigorón stop
Sunset return through the cathedral square and side streets

Ometepe Island.name: Ometepe Island
Ometepe Island.note: Pick a base carefully because cross-island travel is slower than it looks; Moyogalpa is easiest for ferries, while Balgüe and Santo Domingo are better if hikes and springs matter more than quick departures.

activities.name: San Jorge–Moyogalpa ferry as a foot passenger
activities.tip: Slow ferries are cheapest, while faster launches cost more and can sell out first on Friday and Sunday crossings.

activities.name: Ojo de Agua spring pools
Charco Verde nature reserve
Punta Jesús María sunset walk

hotels.name: Finca San Juan de la Isla

activities.name: Volcán Maderas guided hike with crater-lagoon route
activities.tip: Typical setup is about 120 NIO for entry plus a local guide fee that drops sharply if you share the hike with a group.

activities.name: Volcán Concepción guided ascent
activities.tip: Expect roughly a ₹425 park fee plus the certified guide cost; solo hikers pay more than shared groups.

activities.name: El Ceibo Museum as a lower-effort cultural alternative
Lakefront recovery stop around Santo Domingo after the hike

San Juan del Sur.name: San Juan del Sur
San Juan del Sur.note: Town is compact and easiest on foot, but beach-hopping beyond the bay usually means shuttle timing, motorbike rental, or negotiating a taxi in advance.

activities.name: Mirador del Cristo de la Misericordia
San Juan del Sur bay promenade and town beach walk
Taxi ride or steep walk up to the Cristo hill
Sunset cocktail or seafood dinner on the waterfront

days.theme: Playa Maderas Surf Day or Catamaran Trip

activities.name: Casa Oro shuttle or local shuttle to Playa Maderas
Playa Maderas surf lesson with board rental
Taxi to Playa Maderas if you miss the shuttle
Group catamaran or sunset sailing trip

connections.method: Chicken buses from Mercado Huembes or the UCA area run frequently; taxis and private transfers are much faster if you arrive late or carry bags
Take the public bus back through Managua for the cheapest run, or book a shared tourist shuttle if you want a simpler same-day intercity transfer
Most budget travelers use buses via Managua and Rivas, then a taxi or tuk-tuk to San Jorge port and the ferry to Moyogalpa; shared shuttles cost more but save hassle
Take the ferry back to San Jorge, continue by taxi or local bus to Rivas, then hop on the short chicken bus or taxi onward to San Juan del Sur

extras: December to April is the cleanest weather window for combining Masaya crater visits, León side trips, Ometepe hikes, and San Juan del Sur surf beaches in one loop.
Carry small córdobas because chicken buses, ferries, reserve entrances, and many taxi rides are still easier to pay in cash than by card.
If you only have a week, cut either León or San Juan del Sur; trying to keep both plus Ometepe usually turns into too many long transfer days.
